channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: All eyes on Orion’s heat shield: Artemis 2 astronauts will hit Earth's atmosphere at nearly 24,000 mph on April 10 - Space
item.description:
1. All eyes on Orion’s heat shield: Artemis 2 astronauts will hit Earth's atmosphere at nearly 24,000 mph on April 10  Space
  2. ‘It really bent your mind’: The life-altering phenomenon astronauts experience in space  CNN
  3. For Artemis II, Returning to Earth May Be the Most Dangerous Part of the Mission  The New York Times
  4. Artemis II Astronauts Witnessed 6 Meteorites Colliding With the Moon  WIRED
  5. A cracked heat shield rattled NASA after Artemis I. Now, Artemis II will put the fix to the test  Los Angeles Times

item.title: A San Diego amphibious ship and its crew are about to become the final stop between four astronauts and solid ground when Artemis II splashes down on Friday - supercarblondie.com
item.description: A San Diego amphibious ship and its crew are about to become the final stop between four astronauts and solid ground when Artemis II splashes down on Friday  supercarblondie.comView Full Coverage on Google News

item.title: "Weird blob" creature thought to be the world's oldest octopus isn't an octopus after all. Here's what scientists found. - CBS News
item.description:
1. "Weird blob" creature thought to be the world's oldest octopus isn't an octopus after all. Here's what scientists found.  CBS News
  2. The ‘oldest octopus’ in the world isn’t an octopus after all, scientists find  CNN
  3. This Fossil Once Rewrote Everything We Knew About Octopuses. Turns Out, It's Not An Octopus At All  IFLScience
  4. So long, sucker: world’s ‘oldest octopus’ is stripped of its title  The Times
  5. This Record-Breaking Octopus Fossil Isn’t an Octopus After All  Nautilus | Science

item.title: Is the next 'great comet' coming? See Comet PanSTARRS brighten in the night sky this week. - Live Science
item.description:

item.title: One DNA letter can trigger complete sex reversal - Phys.org
item.description:

item.title: The lengths male octopuses go to protect the arm they need to mate - Phys.org
item.description:
1. The lengths male octopuses go to protect the arm they need to mate  Phys.org
  2. Male octopus has ‘sex arm’ that can mate in the dark  National Geographic
  3. How octopuses have sex without even seeing each other  Euronews.com
  4. The One Arm an Octopus Will Never Risk in a Fight—Its Sex Arm  ZME Science
  5. A Harvard researcher started studying how octopuses use receptors in their arms to “taste by touch” while hunting prey and exploring. In doing so, his team stumbled on answers to the birds and the bees of cephalopods getting busy. https://wapo.st/4dNeHTR  facebook.com

item.title: This Company Is Launching 4,000 Orbiting ‘Sky Mirrors’ to Beam Sunlight Down to Earth After Dark. Scientists Want It Stopped Now - Indian Defence Review
item.description:
